behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: could look like parser logic (Unit/Integration). Pass 2: the behavior is explicitly about a real git repository classification of renames, which depends on git rename detection and CLI subprocess output shape. MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: bug can appear at the git subprocess + parser boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text says files are 'renamed' in repository analysis; without real git rename semantics, a fake can hide misclassification.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real git repo, commit a file, rename it, and run the CLI against that repo via subprocess. Assert report has the file only in `renamed`, and not in `added` or `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: could parse mocked diff text (Integration). Pass 2: real failure commonly comes from actual git binary diff/subprocess bytes/encoding behavior, which mocks smooth over. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both point to System. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires binary diffs from real repository content; fake payloads may not reproduce real subprocess encoding/format edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real git repo with at least one binary file change, run the CLI as a subprocess, assert exit code is success and binary file count/category is present in stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: might be exception mapping logic (Integration). Pass 2: this behavior depends on real git repo state with unborn HEAD and real git exit codes/messages. REAL-INFRA => System. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text targets a repository with no commits; the failure boundary is real git command behavior in that state.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh git repo with zero commits, run the CLI process, assert no crash/non-error exit and stdout is an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: could test counting function with fixtures (Unit/Integration). Pass 2: behavior explicitly validates against actual repository history, so real git history traversal and subprocess output must be exercised. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed histories can pass while real command interactions fail. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text says 'actual change history of the repository', which disqualifies pure in-process fakes as minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp git repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run the CLI, and assert category counts exactly match expected totals from that real history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: could compare two internal code paths with mocks (Integration). Pass 2: staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/working-tree behaviors exposed through real subprocess calls, so minimum boundary is System. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly contrasts staged and unstaged repository states; real git index behavior is the failure surface.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, make equivalent file edits in two scenarios (staged vs unstaged), run CLI in each scenario, and assert normalized stdout reports are identical.
